EVEN IF BURDEN WAS SUSTAINED, DEFENDANT IS NOT ESTOPPED FROM DEMURRING TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION. "'BENNETT ALSO AFFIRMED THE PRINCIPLE THAT WHEN A PLAINTIFF FILES AN AMENDED PLEADING IN RESPONSE TO AN ORDER SUSTAINING A PRIOR DEMURRER TO A CAUSE OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND, THE AMENDED CAUSE OF ACTION IS TREATED AS A NEW PLEADING AND A DEFENDANT IS FREE TO RESPOND TO IT BY DEMURRER ON ANY GROUND." BERG & BERG ENTERPRISES, LLC V. BOYLE (2009) 178 CAL.APP.4TH 1020, DISCUSSING BENNETT V. SUNCLOUD (1997) 56 CAL.APP.4TH 91. ADDITIONALLY, SEPTEMBER 5, 2012 ORDER DID NOT ADDRESS THE ISSUE OF RELIANCE AS DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION DID NOT RAISE THIS ARGUMENT. (SEE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT 3:25-5:8). PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, CLERK J. Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Court'S Order Sustaining Def Chicago Title Insurance Company'S Demurrer To Pltff'S Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFs DANIEL WOODY, VORNG TYRON, ROXANA GUARDIA, UNA TRAYNOR, RONELLE LEE and DIANE LOPEZ Motion For Reconsideration Of Court'S Order Sustaining Defendant Chicago Title Insurance Company'S Demurrer To Plaintiff's Third Amended Complaint is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to sustain burden under CCP 1008. Plaintiff's counsel states in her declaration that she was present at August 5, 2013 hearing and an oral argument took place. (Noll Declaration 3). No explanation is provided for failure to bring up the collateral estoppel argument at that time. Even if burden was sustained, Defendant is not estopped from demurring to the cause of action for Intentional Misrepresentation. "'Bennett also affirmed the principle that when a plaintiff files an amended pleading in response to an order sustaining a prior demurrer to a cause of action with leave to amend, the amended cause of action is treated as a new pleading and a defendant is free to respond to it by demurrer on any ground." Berg & Berg Enterprises, LLC v. Boyle (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 1020, discussing Bennett v. Suncloud (1997) 56 Cal.App.4th 91. Additionally, September 5, 2012 order did not address the issue of reliance as Defendant's demurrer to cause of action for Intentional Misrepresentation did not raise this argument. (See Points and Authorities in Support of Demurrer to First Amended Complaint 3:25-5:8). Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk J. Rumsey, Not Reported. (501)
